specific_location: Black Canyon of the Gunnison National Park
description: The Painted Redstart was repeatedly making single-syllable chirps while moving its body.  It would turn back and forth with each chirp, and move its tail and wings at the same time.  This is how I noticed it because it was deep in the shrubbery, but constantly moving.  When I first noticed it, several Stellar's Jays seemed interested in it and had flown to the same shrubbery, but were not outright harassing it.  The jays left, and the Painted Redstart continued on with its movement and calling.  After several minutes it began to fly around in the shrubbery. After approximately 15 minutes the Painted Redstart flew further away and out of sight.
